name: Favorable traits of character.
section: W0. Favorable traits of character.

name: Good inclinations enter body at puberty.
section: W0. Favorable traits of character.

name: Kindness. (Cf. Q40.)
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Saint never drives fly or gnat from his face.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Saint gives calf to wolf.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Generosity.
section: W10. Kindness.
bibliographies: Irish myth: *Cross; Spanish: Keller, Espinosa Jr. No. 200; Jewish: Neuman; India: Thompson-Balys.

name: Philanthropist will give his spurs if someone will drive his horse for him. He has given away his coat, etc. to beggars. One finally asks for his spurs.
section: W10. Kindness.
additional_description: He has given away his coat, etc. to beggars. One finally asks for his spurs.

name: Munificent monarch.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Emperor thinks day lost when he gives no gifts.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: King will not eat breakfast till gifts are given.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Man divides money into three parts: (1) for the poor, (2) for pilgrims, (3) for himself and family.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Man lets himself be sold as slave so as to practice generosity.
section: W10. Kindness.
bibliographies: Wesselski Archiv Orientální II 429; Italian Novella: Rotunda.

name: Brahmin steals to feed guests (deities).
section: W10. Kindness.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: U.P. Mirzapur : NQ III No. 132 .

name: Generosity toward enemy.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Ruler pardons his enemies.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Man pardons his assailants. They attack again and are killed.
section: W10. Kindness.
additional_description: They attack again and are killed.

name: Ruler pardons emissary who has come to murder him.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Wronged knight conquers enemy and then pardons him.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Conqueror returns defeated man's wife and family to him.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Conqueror spares city.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Ruler reproaches son for bringing him the head of his fallen enemy. Latter given honorable burial.
section: W10. Kindness.
additional_description: Latter given honorable burial.

name: Man saved through intercession of enemy.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Man pays fine for enemy who is falsely accused.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Captor contributes to captive's ransom.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Warrior gives steed to his enemy.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Calumniated woman intercedes for accusers. (Cf. K2112.)
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Man's life spared through the intercession of the daughter he has wronged. He has ordered her killed.
section: W10. Kindness.
additional_description: He has ordered her killed.

name: Champion grants requested boon of safe convoy to his enemies.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Chieftain spares enemies whom he takes unawares (unarmed).
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Chieftain who takes king unawares, spares him on condition that chieftain be called "lover" by women of king's tribe.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Hero in battle refuses to slay charioteers, women, or physicians. (Cf. C867.1.)
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Ruler generously settles quarrel between loser and finder of money.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Nobleman's generosity enables impoverished lover to entertain his lady.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Impoverished nobleman offers wife to ruler. Latter spares her honor and aids the couple.
section: W10. Kindness.
additional_description: Latter spares her honor and aids the couple.

name: General sends for king so that the latter may get credit for victory.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Prince donates all including a tooth. When he has nothing left he is asked for a gift. He allows man to extract a tooth from his mouth. (Father had offered reward to anyone persuading his son to have it extracted.)
section: W10. Kindness.
additional_description: When he has nothing left he is asked for a gift. He allows man to extract a tooth from his mouth. (Father had offered reward to anyone persuading his son to have it extracted.)

name: Ruler protects thief. Asks for the stolen plate and hides it so that it will not be found on the culprit.
section: W10. Kindness.
additional_description: Asks for the stolen plate and hides it so that it will not be found on the culprit.

name: Ruler rewards man who would curb him.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: The generous correction. Ruler asks treasurer to give man 200 crowns. Treasurer makes error and puts down 300. Ruler raises sum to 400. "I can't allow your pen to be more liberal than I."
section: W10. Kindness.
additional_description: Ruler asks treasurer to give man 200 crowns. Treasurer makes error and puts down 300. Ruler raises sum to 400. "I can't allow your pen to be more liberal than I." Italian Novella:

name: King rewards knight with large sum of money. Chancellor: "If you saw such a sum it would seem large to you." King answers that it looks small and doubles it.
section: W10. Kindness.
additional_description: Chancellor: "If you saw such a sum it would seem large to you." King answers that it looks small and doubles it.

name: Novel sign of death. It is the first time that what he has given away seems too much.
section: W10. Kindness.
additional_description: It is the first time that what he has given away seems too much.

name: Youngest brother shares wealth with older brothers who foolishly lost theirs. (Cf. L31.)
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Generous person refuses no man anything. (Cf. C871.)
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Generous king gives away his only eye.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Hospitality as a virtue. (Cf. P320.)
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Man (king) prefers death to loss of reputation for hospitality.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Man looks for strangers so as to bestow hospitality on them.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Woman shelters son's murderer out of charity.
section: W10. Kindness.
bibliographies: Spanish: Boggs FFC XC 86 No. 756D*; Italian Novella: *Rotunda.

name: Murderer goes to victim's son and asks to be killed. The son aids him to escape.
section: W10. Kindness.
additional_description: The son aids him to escape.

name: Bishop exchanges places with prisoner so as to have him return to his mother.
section: W10. Kindness.

name: Envious man sets out to kill one who is noted for his generosity. He is won over by the generosity of his intended victim.
section: W10. Kindness.
additional_description: He is won over by the generosity of his intended victim.

name: Other favorable traits of character.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Emperor friendly to everyone. Would act as he should like an emperor to act toward him if he were a common man.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.
additional_description: Would act as he should like an emperor to act toward him if he were a common man.

name: Man never listens to scandal.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Man speaks no evil.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Equanimity of the comedian. When he sees his house carried off by a flood, he fiddles the whole time.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.
additional_description: When he sees his house carried off by a flood, he fiddles the whole time.

name: Equanimity of the enslaved unfortunate. Does not complain when beset by a series of misfortunes. (Cf. N251.1.)
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.
additional_description: Does not complain when beset by a series of misfortunes.

name: Minister always says, "It is for the best," when anything happens.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: MADRAS . Natesa Sastri Folk - Tales 274 .

name: Patience.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Gratitude.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Man weeps with gratitude to God because He did not make him hideous as toad.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Self-sacrifice.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Woman drinks poison that son may be king.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Woman sacrifices life for son's honor.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Man leaps from vessel into jaws of sea-beast so as to save remaining passengers.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Saint threatens to take place of homicide in hell unless soul is released.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Obedience.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Bravery.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: King Richard leads host to victory by leaping his horse overboard.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Heroism.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Badly wounded warrior continues fighting.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Warrior refuses to delay battle in return for promise of long, happy life.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Loyalty.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Man constrained to flee with leader's fiancée resists her blandishments.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Loyal friends refuse to fight against disgraced comrade. (Cf. P310.)
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Cowardly to leave battle while lord is alive.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Fakir rewarded by prince with a life of luxury in city chooses to return to his monkey friends in jungle.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: PUNJAB . Crooke Ind . Antiq . XXIX 361 .

name: Justice.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Man picked to judge bull-fight because he is "no fairer to his friend than to his foe."
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Upright judge refuses a bribe: he is responsible to a higher tribunal called Conscience.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Conscientiousness.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Man never breaks his word.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Oil well digger runs out of money, leaves the country, forgetting to tell his driller. He returns fifty years later, finds the driller still drilling away.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.
additional_description: He returns fifty years later, finds the driller still drilling away.

name: Man keeps promise to return to be eaten by tiger: let go without harm.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Rabbi returns jewel found with bought ass. He had not paid for it.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.
additional_description: He had not paid for it.

name: Peacefulness.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Honor.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.

name: Prostrate soldier asks his enemy to stab him in the chest instead of the back in order to save his honor. The captor frees him and the two become friends.
section: W20. Other favorable traits of character.
additional_description: The captor frees him and the two become friends.

name: Unfavorable traits of character.
section: W100. Unfavorable traits of character.

name: Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Laziness.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Types 368*, 675, 822, 843*, 1370*, 1405, 1454*, 1561, 1950; *Chauvin IX 33f. Nos. 25, 26; *Krappe Bulletin Hispanique XXXIX 30; Missouri French: Carrière; S. Am. Indian (Chiriguano): Métraux RMLP XXXIII 175; West Indies: Flowers 581.

name: Contest in laziness. Each cites instances of his laziness.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: *Type 1950; *BP III 207; Wesselski Märchen 213 No. 21; Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 261; Oesterley No. 91; Fb "doven" IV 102b. Spanish Exempla: Keller; India: Thompson-Balys.
additional_description: Each cites instances of his laziness.

name: Man lets legs burn in fire rather than move them.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Wesselski Märchen 213 No. 21; Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 261; India: Thompson-Balys.

name: Man is burned to death because he is too lazy to put out spark.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Lazy man's belongings burn. He is too lazy to stop rats from playing with lighted candle.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: He is too lazy to stop rats from playing with lighted candle.

name: Man too lazy to open eyes to see where fire is burning.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Three lazy beggars burn alive rather than run away from burning castle.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Mother calls to daughter to tell her that she (daughter) is standing on a live coal. The daughter asks which foot the coal is under.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: The daughter asks which foot the coal is under.

name: Man will not lift knife to cut rope about to hang him.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Wesselski Märchen 213 No. 21; Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 261.

name: Man will not move in bed when water drops in his eyes.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Wesselski Märchen 213 No. 21; Spanish Exempla: Keller; Italian Novella: Rotunda.

name: Man so lazy that he will not wipe his running nose. This causes him to lose prospective bride.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: This causes him to lose prospective bride.

name: Man floating in river too lazy to drink. His voice is damaged.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: His voice is damaged.

name: Man too lazy to pick up berries (dates).
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: GANGES VALLEY . Shyama - Shankar 106. - BOMBAY . Jethabhai 28f . No. 9 .

name: The lazy servant.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Lazy servant gets others to perform his duties.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: GANGES VALLEY . Shyama- Shankar 191f .

name: Servant to call his master at daybreak: looks into dark closet to see if it is yet light. They sleep till noon.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: They sleep till noon.

name: Servant to close door at night: leaves it open so that he will not have to open it next morning.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: "If it is day, give me food; if it is night, let me sleep." The master has told the servant to go to work.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: The master has told the servant to go to work.

name: Boy to see whether it is raining: calls dog (cat) in and feels of his paws.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Wesselski Mönchslatein 54 No. 47; *Crane Vitry 216 No. 204; Spanish Exempla: Keller; India: Thompson-Balys: GANGES VALLEY . Shyama - Shankar 65 .

name: Boy to see whether there is fire in the house: feels of cat to see if she is warm.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: The boy eats breakfast, dinner, and supper one immediately after the other; then lies down to sleep.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Lazy boy always says that it is raining. The peasant and the boy sleep during the rain in the hay barn. When the peasant asks, the boy always says that it is still raining.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: The peasant and the boy sleep during the rain in the hay barn. When the peasant asks, the boy always says that it is still raining.

name: Lazy boy says he cannot walk.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Servant tells master to cover his face: no need to put out lamp.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: The lazy wife.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Lazy wife in soiled dress thinks husband is bringing her a new dress from market. He is bringing a goose.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: He is bringing a goose.

name: Cat beaten for not working. Lazy wife must hold cat and is scratched.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: Lazy wife must hold cat and is scratched.

name: Lazy wife throws bread out of window instead of putting it back into oven.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Why he beats her. Lazy wife beaten by husband maintains that she has done nothing. That is why he is beating her.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: Lazy wife beaten by husband maintains that she has done nothing. That is why he is beating her.

name: Wife too lazy to spin. Supposed dead husband comes back to life and gives his wife a beating, because there was no shirt even in the event of death.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 1370B*; Russian: Andrejev No. 1370B*.
additional_description: Supposed dead husband comes back to life and gives his wife a beating, because there was no shirt even in the event of death.

name: "Who will not work, shall not eat." Idle daughter-in-law learns work.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: Idle daughter-in-law learns work.

name: Lazy husband.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Other lazy persons.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Lazy mother given shoes of cotton; son knows that she will not wear them out.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Lazy girl does not know where the spring is.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Lazy man asked direction only points with his foot.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Lazy dog wakes only for his meals.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 73 (ET 381), 126 (ST 348); Halm Aesop No. 413.

name: Man in mud too lazy to take hand extended to help him up.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Lazy son-in-law: afraid of a dog.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Ruler is too lazy to stop quarrels. They lead to his death.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: They lead to his death.

name: Man with stolen fig in his mouth submits to having cheek lanced rather than open his mouth. (Cf. J1842.2.)
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Man is so lazy that he starves rather than open his mouth for food to fall in when it falls from trees.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Learning a trade in bed. Working independently, the lazy fellow spoils the materials received – starts making something big, which at the end turns to nothing. For example, begins with forging a plough: this becomes an axe, the axe a knife, and knife a needle, the needle – nothing. (Cf. J2080.)
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: Working independently, the lazy fellow spoils the materials received starts making something big, which at the end turns to nothing. For example, begins with forging a plough: this becomes an axe, the axe a knife, and knife a needle, the needle nothing.

name: Lazy man asks if wood is split before he accepts it as a gift.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Lazy man is being taken to poorhouse or out of town or to cemetery to be buried alive. The group take pity on him, offer him various articles to help him get started again. One offers a bushel of corn. The lazy one rises up from the bottom of the wagon or coffin where he has been lying: "Is the corn shelled?"
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: The group take pity on him, offer him various articles to help him get started again. One offers a bushel of corn. The lazy one rises up from the bottom of the wagon or coffin where he has been lying: "Is the corn shelled?"

name: Lazy man misses seeing the sheriff's funeral; he is facing the wrong way as the procession passes.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Man digs three potatoes in one day: one dug, one being dug, one about to be dug.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Man weeds garden from cushioned rocking chair, using fire tongs to reach weeds.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Slovenliness. (Cf. Q322.)
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: The slovenly fiancée.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Woman becomes clean only after three washings and the use of three pounds of soap.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Rancher is not recognized by his wife and family after he has cleaned up in town at hotel.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Vanity.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Irish myth: Cross; India: Thompson-Balys: INDIA . Raju Fables 30f . - PUNJAB . Steel Punjab 144

name: Old woman reaches town before vain fop who stops to adjust clothes.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Expenditure of money for vanity.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Plain people made rulers have vain display.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Peacock admires self in mirror.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Wasp seeking fame stings courtiers: killed.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Wealthy man pretends enjoyment of good music. Told when to applaud.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: Told when to applaud.

name: Use of strange language to show one's high education.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Jackal forces all animals who come to drink to praise him like a king.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: PUNJAB . Steel Punjab 144 ; Steel - Temple 153 .

name: Boastfulness.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Neglected wife given trifle boasts of it.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Cowardice.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Irish myth: *Cross; Missouri French: Carrière; Icelandic: *Boberg.

name: Hunter wants to be shown lion tracks, not lion himself.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 67 (ET 314), 101 (ST 143); Halm Aesop No. 114.

name: Coward boasts when there is no danger. (Cf. J974, J978.)
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Ass insults dying lion.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 56 (ET 166), 112 (ST 233); Jacobs Aesop 202 No. 9; Spanish Exempla: Keller.

name: Fox insults caged lion.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 56 (ET 160), 112 (ST 232); Halm Aesop No. 40.

name: Crow sits on sheep's back; afraid to sit on dog's.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Dogs tear up lion skin: fear living lion.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 55 (ET 153), 112 (ST 235); Halm Aesop No. 219.

name: Coward gloats over robber slain by another person.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Monkey safe in tree insults gorilla as "broken face".
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Cowardly soldier turns back when he hears raven's croak.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 73 (ET 385), 137 (ST 425); Halm Aesop No. 379.

name: Queen mother shames cowardly son and companions. "In truth, gentlemen, you do well in weeping; for since you didn't fight like men to defend your land, it is suitable that now you weep like women on leaving it."
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: "In truth, gentlemen, you do well in weeping; for since you didn't fight like men to defend your land, it is suitable that now you weep like women on leaving it."

name: Cowardly spider rushes at fly but hides when wasp appears.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Coward gives his purse to thief with lame excuse.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Cowardly bridegroom flees elephant and loses bride.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Illness from fear.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Swooning from cowardice.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: MADRAS . Natesa Sastri Dravidian Nights 211

name: Indecision. (Cf. J1040.)
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: The man who only tasted wine. Will not drink but gets drunk nevertheless by frequent tasting.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: Will not drink but gets drunk nevertheless by frequent tasting.

name: Gluttony.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Jewish: *Neuman; India: Thompson-Balys: ASSAM HILLS . Khasi : Rafy 111. - CEYLON . Parker I 320 ; ІІ 186 ; Ш 289; N. Am. Indian (California): Gayton and Newman 67.

name: Greedy animal eats poisoned fruit in spite of warning.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Gluttonous wife eats all the meal while cooking it.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: INDIA . Venkataswami 80f . -MADRAS . Natesa Sastri Folklore 293 ; Tamil : Kingscote 261f . - CEYLON . Parker ІІ 207 ; III 101 .

name: New son-in-law given choice of meals eats all.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: MYSORE . Komati : Rao , C. 9

name: Pupil eats cakes given as alms for his master.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Husband eats wife's share of food as well as his own.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: U.P. Oudh : Crooke- Rouse 61f . No. 15. — BOMBAY . New Goa : Davidson - Phelps 40 No. 16a , 41 No. 16b

name: [First Edition: W125.1. "I commend my family to your care", says the glutton as he begins eating a dish he has been warned will kill him.]
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Disobedience.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Missouri French: Carrière; Spanish Exempla: Keller; Jewish: Neuman.

name: Monk obedient only as long as work is agreeable. Always says that unpleasant work is beneath his dignity.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: Always says that unpleasant work is beneath his dignity.

name: Petulance.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Dissatisfaction.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Hog tired of his daily food. Goes to the judge and gets better food assigned to him, but the fox cheats him out of it.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: Goes to the judge and gets better food assigned to him, but the fox cheats him out of it.

name: Dissatisfied fox. In kingdom of lions laments that he always gets the worst bits of food.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: In kingdom of lions laments that he always gets the worst bits of food.

name: Dissatisfied rivers complain against sea. Say that it makes their water unusable.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI *75 (ET 408), 125 (ST 344); Halm Aesop No 380.
additional_description: Say that it makes their water unusable.

name: Peacock dissatisfied with his voice.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Monk dissatisfied with things as he has them is admonished to take what he receives from God.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Sparrow dissatisfied with pond water wants to go to sea.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Profligacy.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Profligate wastes entire fortune before beginning his own adventures.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Inconsistency.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Prince penalizes cursing, although he himself curses.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Woman admires marvelous shot of hero which kills deer, but angers him when she begs mercy for the deer.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: False modesty.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Look! look! she cries from the barrel. A nobleman has arranged for a peasant girl to become his mistress. When he comes to take her away he cannot find her. Disappointed, he is about to depart when the girl, who has hidden in a barrel, calls out "Look!" She really wants to be found.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.
additional_description: look! she cries from the barrel. A nobleman has arranged for a peasant girl to become his mistress. When he comes to take her away he cannot find her. Disappointed, he is about to depart when the girl, who has hidden in a barrel, calls out "Look!" She really wants to be found.

name: Talkativeness.
section: W110. Unfavorable traits of character – personal.

name: Greed.

name: Cauldron of greed.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Harlot weeps when her impoverished lover leaves her to think that she has left him his coat.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
bibliographies: *Crane Vitry 214 No. 200; Herbert III 16; *Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 10; Scala Celi 87b No. 512; Italian Novella: Rotunda.

name: Visiting friends take everything from house of dying man.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Visitors of sick stag eat up all his provisions so that he starves.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI *60 (ET 227), 122 (ST 311); Halm Aesop No. 131.

name: Hospitable man impoverished by greedy guests.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Wolves devour an ox without leaving a share for the rightful owner.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Snake and weasel stop fighting in order to catch mouse.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 49 (ET 80), 134 (ST 396); Halm Aesop No. 345.

name: Father-in-law sued for breach of contract because he does not die in two years as he has predicted.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Hog with broken leg refuses to tell another where peas are to be found. "I intend to go there myself as soon as I get well again."
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
additional_description: "I intend to go there myself as soon as I get well again."

name: Eats up the money. Old man before dying eats his money. In church, son wants to cut corpse open and get the money. The devil: "Don't spoil the skin." Shakes money out of the corpse's throat and takes the skin.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
bibliographies: Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 3621, Legends No. 638.
additional_description: Old man before dying eats his money. In church, son wants to cut corpse open and get the money. The devil: "Don't spoil the skin." Shakes money out of the corpse's throat and takes the skin.

section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Greedy person (animal) gets hand (head) stuck in food jar.

name: Greedy man tries to stuff food into his nostrils as well as into his mouth.

name: Fox spoils his food rather than divide with ape.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Man had rather be burned alive than to share food with a guest.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Stingy dead woman raises her head to correct account of laundress, who is overcharging her daughter.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Stingy man forced to share his money when he lies and says he has none.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Stingy woman will not give soup to man until she spills it. Then she says he may have the soup.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
additional_description: Then she says he may have the soup.

name: Stingy king will not hire soldiers: defeated.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Spider in stingy woman's house grows thin.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Stingy horse refuses ass little feed, though he promises much for later time.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Stingy man cancels invitations to his guests. "It is better that they speak ill of me on an empty stomach than on a full one."
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
additional_description: "It is better that they speak ill of me on an empty stomach than on a full one."

name: Drummer drums for own wedding so as to save expense.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Stingy men love possessions so much that they wear out their feet to save shoes.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Stingy man and his servants.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Farmer gets help up early in morning for a light breakfast: a glass of water and a lantern.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Stingy farmer encourages help by promise of hot lunch. The servant discovers that the hot lunch is a mustard sandwich.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
additional_description: The servant discovers that the hot lunch is a mustard sandwich.

name: Master insists that maid whistle when she brings in the dessert. He is afraid she will eat the raisins out of the cakes.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
additional_description: He is afraid she will eat the raisins out of the cakes.

name: Master insists that servants whistle as they pick strawberries so that they cannot eat any.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: The stingy man and his animals.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: A stranger notices that man's cows need feed, feeds them. They die of the shock of being fed.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
additional_description: They die of the shock of being fed.

name: Man who insists on using everything that is useful.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Man finds a bunghole lying around loose, has cooper build a barrel around it.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Man saves sausage skins, sends them back for refilling.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Stingy man does not eat butter; only looks at it and enjoys the thought.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Wife of stingy man prays that her husband become sick so that she can get better food.

name: Wife keeps half of the money she plans to give for a shrine.

name: Miser's heart found in his strong-box.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
additional_description: Gets burned in the chimney and beaten in the bed where he hides.

name: Miserly husband spies on wife, lest she cook too much.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Miserly wife exposed to guests by her husband.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Man is so miserly that he never drinks wine until it becomes strong. Gets full benefit from it.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
additional_description: Gets full benefit from it.

name: Friar so miserly that he stays in ditch three days before "lending" a hand to his rescuers.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Miser dreams that he spends some money. Strangles himself in his sleep.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Miser decides not to commit suicide. Does not want the expense of a rope.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
additional_description: Does not want the expense of a rope.

name: Miser is given rope to hang himself. Miser annoys merchant so much over the price of a rope that the latter gives it to him provided he will hang himself as he plans to do.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
additional_description: Miser annoys merchant so much over the price of a rope that the latter gives it to him provided he will hang himself as he plans to do.

name: Miser appoints himself as the sole heir of his own estate.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Miser decides to kill his pigs himself. Refuses to pay fee. Chain of circumstances make it infinitely more expensive.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Miser is rebuked by friend. "You get no benefit from your wealth." Proves his folly.

name: Miser dies because he will not buy a candle.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Dying miser tells son to extinguish candle just as soon as he dies.

name: Grateful animals; ungrateful man. A traveler saves a monkey, a snake, a tiger, and a jeweler from a pit. The monkey gives him fruit; the tiger a necklace of a princess he has killed. name: Sheep jealous of dog because he does nothing. Do not consider that he guards the flock.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI *58 (ET 197), 132 (ST 386); Halm Aesop No. 317.
additional_description: Do not consider that he guards the flock.

name: King kills architect after completion of great building, so that he may never again build one so great. (Cf. S161.0.1, W154.21.)
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Architect kills pupil who has surpassed him in skill.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Architect commits suicide when he discovers that his pupil has surpassed him in skill.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Raven wants to be as white as a swan.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI *46 (ET 49), 90 (ST 26); Halm Aesop No. 206.

name: Jealous fox betrays wolf to peasant and then appropriates wolf's cave and food. Peasant kills him in a few days.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
additional_description: Peasant kills him in a few days.

name: Raven jealous of partridge's way of flying.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Jealousy of Venus in the love of Psyche and Cupid.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Ruler who is jealous of his subjects' happiness prohibits their games.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: The crying child. He stops crying so that after a rest he can cry louder than ever.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
bibliographies: *Crane Vitry 265 No. 300; Herbert III 13 No. 85; *Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 594; Scala Celi 158b No. 892.
additional_description: He stops crying so that after a rest he can cry louder than ever.

name: Violence of temper.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Man demonstrates his violence of temper. He overhears a man tell of his temper. In anger he enters and demands to know when he has ever lost his temper.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
additional_description: He overhears a man tell of his temper. In anger he enters and demands to know when he has ever lost his temper.

name: Prayer that overbearing knight's illness be increased. A little sickness has made him kind; more may make him kinder.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
bibliographies: Crane Vitry 48 No. 103; Mensa Philosophica No. 143.
additional_description: A little sickness has made him kind; more may make him kinder.

name: Temper lost from reading history. Man so angered that he refuses to pay his workmen.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
additional_description: Man so angered that he refuses to pay his workmen.

name: Monk loses temper at cup and breaks it. [Inadvertant duplication of H1553.5.]
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Violence of judge's temper leads him to have men given death sentence unjustly.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Insult worse than wound. The lion to the man: "The wound has healed, but the pain of harsh words still remains."
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.
bibliographies: Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 161*; Rumanian: Schullerus FFC LXXVIII No. 159IV*; India: Thompson-Balys.
additional_description: The lion to the man: "The wound has healed, but the pain of harsh words still remains."

name: Insolence.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Contentiousness. (Cf. Q300.)
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Extortion.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Man extorts large price for betel leaf from addict.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Envy. (Cf. Q302.)
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Goose and turkey envious of peacock point out its ugly legs and voice.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Lack of patience.
section: W150. Unfavorable traits of character – social.

name: Traits of character – miscellaneous.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.

name: Active imagination.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.

name: The boy: "If I had one and then got two more, I should have three."
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.

name: "I surely saw a hundred wolves (snakes)." – "There weren't so many as that." – "Well, what made the noise in the bushes?"
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.
bibliographies: Type 2009*; Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 1863*; Estonian: Aarne FFC XXV No. 2009*; Russian: Andrejev No. 2009; India: *Thompson-Balys.
additional_description: "There weren't so many as that." "Well, what made the noise in the bushes?"

name: "I am not alone!" Man travelling alone through the forest at night, is afraid of robbers. He hangs his cap on a stick and keeps repeating: "I am not travelling alone, there are two of us."
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.
additional_description: Man travelling alone through the forest at night, is afraid of robbers. He hangs his cap on a stick and keeps repeating: "I am not travelling alone, there are two of us."

name: Eagerness for combat.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.

name: Eager warriors go through tent wall. Warriors so eager for battle they do not take time to go out of tent door but go through the tent wall.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.
additional_description: Warriors so eager for battle they do not take time to go out of tent door but go through the tent wall.

name: Warriors contend with each other until battle starts. When they agree to engage in battle, they cannot endure to be without combat.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.
additional_description: When they agree to engage in battle, they cannot endure to be without combat.

name: Man will not allow food served to strangers until a man of them wrestles with him.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.

name: Host requires deed of bravery before feast is eaten.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.

name: Man will not do a woman's bidding.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.

name: Extreme prudence.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.

name: Magic help sent to enemy. Hero sends fairy healing charms to his enemy so that when they resume fighting it cannot be said he wins because of superior care.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.
additional_description: Hero sends fairy healing charms to his enemy so that when they resume fighting it cannot be said he wins because of superior care.

name: Refusal to fight wounded enemy. Hero feels it dishonorable to fight with sorely wounded enemy, because it would be said he died of previous wounds rather than the ones hero might inflict.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.
additional_description: Hero feels it dishonorable to fight with sorely wounded enemy, because it would be said he died of previous wounds rather than the ones hero might inflict.

name: Long life sacrificed that descendants may be kings as prophesied. (Cf. M314.)
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.

name: Thrift.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.

name: Thrifty merchant tells son that even a snake laid by will be useful.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.

name: Taciturn man.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.

name: Man is rebuked for loquaciousness when he speaks after thirty-seven days.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.

name: Moving home simple for poor man. Man is so poor that when he moves all he has to do is to put out the fire and whistle for the dog to follow him.
section: W200. Traits of character – miscellaneous.
additional_description: Man is so poor that when he moves all he has to do is to put out the fire and whistle for the dog to follow him.
